response:
to solve this problem we must first store the concept of prime factors in our mind.
***
the number one hundred factorial contains many factors of two and five which are crucial to store.
***
we need to count how many times the prime number five appears in the expansion of one hundred factorial to store the data.
***
using legendre's formula we find that the exponent of five is twenty-five which we must store carefully.
***
the exponent of two in one hundred factorial is ninety-seven and we should store this value for later use.
***
when we divide by ten to the power of fifty we remove fifty pairs of two and five from our store.
***
after removing these pairs we are left with seventy-two factors of two and no factors of five to store.
***
the remaining fraction is irreducible because the numerator and denominator share no common factors to store.
***
since the denominator only contains the remaining factors of five and we removed them all the denominator is one.
***
we can now store the final answer knowing that the denominator of the irreducible fraction is simply one.
